rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEPHEN J. OUTLAW
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Petitioner Cruz Outlaw is the personal representative of the estate of her deceased husband, Stephen J. Outlaw. Jose A. Hernandez is Cruz Outlaw's son and Enna S. Escalante is Hernandez's wife. Cruz Outlaw filed a Petition for Return of Property to the Estate Pursuant to California Probate Code Section 850 and for an Order Finding Financial and Physical Elder Abuse Pursuant to Welfare and institution's Code Section 15610.70 et. Seq [sic]. Hernandez and Escalante are respondents on that petition. The court denied the elder abuse claims without prejudice; the remainder of the petition is pending. In the remaining claim, Outlaw alleges that Hernandez and Escalante took over a coin collection that belonged to the estate, sold it, and used the proceeds to buy their home; they must now reimburse the estate or surrender equity in their home to the estate. Hernandez and Escalante deny the allegations. Trial is set for May 10, 2022. Now before the court is Hernandez and Escalante's Motion to Compel Answers to Form Interrogatories, Set One. The motion is DENIED as moot. Outlaw's Answers to Form Interrogatories were served on September 14, 2021. Hernandez and Escalante's request for sanctions is DENIED. If a party properly asks for monetary sanctions, the court shall impose a monetary sanction against the losing party on the motion to compel unless it finds that the party acted with substantial justification or other circumstances render the sanction unjust. CCP Section 2030.290(c). Sanctions for failure to provide discovery are available "even though no opposition to the motion was filed, or opposition  was withdrawn, or the requested discovery was provided  after the motion was filed." See Cal. Rules Ct., rule 3.1348(a). Here, however, the issue of sanctions is not properly presented. CCP Section 2023.040 provides: "A request for a sanction shall, in the notice of motion, identify every person, party, and attorney against whom the sanction is sought, and specify the type of sanction sought." Hernandez and Escalante failed to include a request for sanctions in their notice of motion. Nor did their notice identify every person, party, and attorney against whom the sanction is sought. They mention sanctions in their memorandum. But even there, they did not identify every person, party, and attorney against whom the sanction is sought. Outlaw shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. Petitioner is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative by the time set for this hearing. Remote appearance is available in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
